item.title: Israel must pull troops out of Lebanon under deal with U.S., Iran says
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says Israeli troops can't remain in Lebanon under the pending deal with the U.S.

item.title: Starter homes now cost $1 million in hundreds of U.S. cities, Zillow finds
item.description: Online real estate marketplace Zillow found that 242 U.S. cities now have starter homes worth $1 million or more, nearly triple the number of cities reported in 2020. The company defines a starter home as one in the lowest third of home values in a given region. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: How Georgia's GOP candidates plan on tackling affordability
item.description: Voters in Georgia are casting their ballots in two key Republican runoff races on Tuesday. Trump-backed Lieutenant Gov. Burt Jones and billionaire Rick Jackson are vying for the GOP nomination for governor. The winner will face Democrat and former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms in November. Meanwhile, Trump-backed Rep. Mike Collins is facing off with former college football coach Derek Dooley for the GOP nomination for Senate. The winner of that race will take on Sen. Jon Ossoff in November. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Feds reveal new details of alleged plot to attack White House UFC event
item.description: The FBI said it disrupted an attempt to attack Sunday's UFC America 250 event at the White House, with court records detailing an alleged plot to use small drones carrying explosives.

item.title: Redistricting push may give Republicans an edge in midterms, report finds
item.description: A new report from the National Republican Congressional Committee finds that the GOP has gained an edge in the midterms from redistricting. Political strategists Kevin Sheridan and Mo Elleithee join with analysis.

item.title: 242 U.S. cities have $1 million starter homes, Zillow finds
item.description: The dream of home ownership is becoming more elusive for some first-time buyers. Zillow found that a record 242 cities have starter homes valued at $1 million or more. Orphe Divounguy, senior economist at Zillow, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Sen. Raphael Warnock says "it's past time for Congress to assert itself" in Iran war
item.description: Sen. Raphael Warnock of Georgia has been viewed as a rising star in the Democratic Party since he beat Kelly Loeffler for her Senate seat in 2021. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett sat down with Warnock to discuss his new book, "The Crooked Places Made Straight," the Iran war and more.

item.title: Georgia voters casting ballots in GOP Senate and governor runoffs
item.description: Republicans in Georgia will decide their nominees for governor and an open Senate seat on Tuesday. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry reports from Atlanta.

item.title: Trump insists Iran won't get U.S. funds, but details of agreement still unknown
item.description: President Trump spent the second day of the G7 summit touting an initial agreement to end the war with Iran, even though no one has seen the text of it yet. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes and national security contributor Joe Zacks join with more.

item.title: FBI: Suspects in foiled White House UFC plot planned to use small drones with explosives, snipers
item.description: The FBI disrupted an alleged plot targeting the White House UFC event, officials announced Tuesday. CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has the details.

item.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to boost weapons manufacturing
item.description: President Trump has invoked the Defense Production Act to address constraints in the production of munitions, according to a presidential memo released Tuesday.

item.title: Alleged D.C. National Guard shooter could face death penalty with new charges
item.description: New charges were filed against a man accused of fatally shooting one National Guard member and gravely injuring another in Washington, D.C., opening the door for the DOJ to seek the death penalty if he's convicted.

item.title: 2 people shot at Wilmington Hospital as police search for "possible active shooter"
item.description: Two people were shot at Wilmington Hospital in Delaware as the campus remains on lockdown Tuesday, the city said.

item.title: 17 horses killed in barn fire at Saratoga Casino Hotel Harness Track
item.description: The casino said some 350 horses are usually housed in the area, but the fire was contained before it could spread and only affected the one barn.

item.title: Potential first tropical cyclone of the year developing in the Gulf
item.description: Forecasters have issued an advisory for the potential first tropical cyclone of the 2026 hurricane season, warning it could develop into a tropical storm by Wednesday.

item.title: FBI investigating shooting involving federal agents near U.S.-Canada border in Washington
item.description: Bullets flew in a northern Washington State neighborhood on Tuesday as federal agents engaged with a person near the Canadian border. The FBI is now investigating. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Stocks soar on news of U.S., Iran peace deal
item.description: The Dow rose on Tuesday, building on Monday's news that the U.S. and Iran reached a deal to end the war. Senior Barron's writer Laura Sanicola joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Electricity costs expected to hit record this summer, new analysis finds
item.description: A new analysis projects how much Americans will pay for electricity from June to September, depending on their state of residence.

item.title: Yum! Brands sells struggling Pizza Hut in $2.7 billion deal
item.description: The sale will split ownership of the pizza chain between a U.S.-based private equity firm and a Chinese restaurant company.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $20,000 2-year CD account can earn savers now
item.description: A $20,000 2-year CD account could produce a big return for savers. Here's what to know before getting started now.

item.title: Senate rejects latest resolution to limit Trump's Iran war powers
item.description: The Senate narrowly rejected a war powers resolution on Iran as President Trump touts a framework agreement with Tehran to end the monthslong conflict.

item.title: How an Irish village is bringing back smartphone-free childhoods
item.description: A village in Ireland started a community-wide initiative to bring back smartphone-free childhoods. Parents voluntarily agree to hold off on giving their children smartphones until they are in middle school. CBS News' Leigh Kiniry has more.

item.title: What to expect as Kevin Warsh leads his first Fed interest rate meeting
item.description: New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh is stepping in at a critical juncture for the U.S. economy, with inflation at its highest level in more than three years.

item.title: Knicks' Karl-Anthony Towns gets praise from his NBA inspiration after win
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns will "be beloved forever," Magic Johnson said in a surprise congratulatory message on "CBS Mornings" after the New York Knicks won the NBA championship.

item.title: U.S. citizen released, no charges after accusations of abuse in Colombia
item.description: A video of the alleged incident circulated widely on social media, but prosecutors determined "there had been no sexual abuse and no physical violence."

item.title: Dead or stranded: Commercial sailors suffer in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: India alone has more than 18,000 sailors stuck across the Persian Gulf region. Some tell CBS News it feels like they're "in jail."

item.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
item.description: Semyon Skrepetsky was known for performances and artwork attacking Russian President Vladimir Putin​ and other authoritarian leaders.

item.title: "Star Wars" lightsaber and more iconic film props going up for auction
item.description: Luke Skywalker's lightsaber from the "Star Wars" sequel "The Empire Strikes Back" is expected to sell for at least $1 million at an upcoming auction.

item.title: BASE jumping accident kills 2 including well-known extreme athlete Andy Lewis
item.description: A BASE jumping accident in a Utah canyon killed two people including a daredevil athlete best known for performing onstage with Madonna at the 2012 Super Bowl, authorities said.
